proposed_from.topic: Minimax rate for the L=2 longitudinal g-formula outside the sqrt(n) regime: the transition-density smoothness as a first-order structural exponent with no single-time-point counterpart. For psi=E[Y(1,1)] under sequential exchangeability and strict overlap, with unknown alpha_1/alpha_2-Holder propensities, beta-Holder final-period outcome regression, and t-Holder (in h_1, L^1(dh_2)) transition density on [0,1]^d, conjecture the minimax RMSE is n^{-1/2} + n^{-4s_1/(4s_1+d)} + n^{-4s_2/(4s_2+d)} with s_1=(alpha_1+t)/2 and s_2=(alpha_2+beta)/2 -- period 1's index carrying the TRANSITION smoothness t in place of the outcome smoothness beta that governs the L=1 problem of Robins-Li-Tchetgen-van der Vaart -- with sharp elbow: sqrt(n)-estimable iff min{alpha_1+t, alpha_2+beta} >= d/2. Deliver (C1) an attaining cross-fitted sequential-DR/HOIF estimator that eliminates the mixed cross-period remainder arising because m_1hat regresses the estimated pseudo-outcome m_2hat; (C2) the matching lower bound via a CHAINED fuzzy-hypothesis construction carrying the period-1 perturbation in the transition density (sign-paired with the pi_1 perturbation), since m_1=E[m_2|H_1,A_1] is not a free parameter and the L=1 two-factor perturbation of (pi,mu) is legal only under variation independence; (C3) the structural separation, witnessed at large finite t on a specified analytic ball: psi is sqrt(n)-estimable whenever alpha_2+beta >= d/2 for arbitrarily small alpha_1>0. Answers the verbatim open problem of Cinelli-Feller-Imbens-Kennedy-Magliacane-Zubizarreta (arXiv 2508.17099): 'optimality for time-varying treatment effects outside the sqrt(n) regime is entirely open, regardless of the model setup.'
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The kernel is not a generic claim to be the first longitudinal HOIF result. Its proposed additions relative to the disclosed literature are an incoming-state \(C^t([0,1]^d;L^1(dh_2))\) transition class without outgoing-state density smoothness, direct regression of an augmented pseudo-outcome rather than fitting \(g\), a corrected weighted-transport remainder, the \(\alpha_1+t,\alpha_2+\beta\) frontier, and two normalized paired mixtures that keep the induced backward regression lawful. No set-inclusion or dominance claim is made against the Bonvini--Kennedy--Keele abstract.
seed_list: Sharp matched minimax RMSE and root-n elbow for the two-period Markov g-formula, with transition smoothness replacing terminal-outcome smoothness at period one.
A sequential-HOIF correction architecture that cancels the pseudo-outcome-induced mixed remainder under asymmetric stage smoothness.
A chained two-convex-mixture lower bound that perturbs the legal transition law rather than an inadmissibly free first backward regression.
A structural-separation theorem showing finite transition regularity can offset arbitrarily rough first-period propensity estimation.
A slow-rate studentized limit law for sequential projection-HOIF estimators outside the root-n regime.
Adaptive selection of coupled sequential correction orders and projection dimensions over unknown smoothness indices.
A necessary-and-sufficient scalar-versus-multi-index correction-order frontier for recursive HOIF bias cancellation.
A transition-regularity phase diagram separating genuinely compositional bottlenecks from the single-time HOIF submodel.

seed_details.one_line: Sharp matched minimax RMSE and root-n elbow for the two-period Markov g-formula, with transition smoothness replacing terminal-outcome smoothness at period one.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from single-time HOIF theory after treating the first backward regression as an outcome nuisance.
seed_details.rebuttal: That substitution erases the legal composition constraint \(m_1=\int m_2g\); neither the mixed sequential remainder nor its lower-bound perturbation is variation independent.

seed_details.one_line: A sequential-HOIF correction architecture that cancels the pseudo-outcome-induced mixed remainder under asymmetric stage smoothness.
seed_details.referee_objection: Sequential double robustness already controls all longitudinal remainder terms.
seed_details.rebuttal: Its first-order expansion establishes consistency and regular asymptotics, not projection corrections for the recursively estimated pseudo-outcome.

seed_details.one_line: A chained two-convex-mixture lower bound that perturbs the legal transition law rather than an inadmissibly free first backward regression.
seed_details.referee_objection: The existing two-mixture device applies unchanged after relabeling the first regression.
seed_details.rebuttal: Relabeling produces alternatives outside the longitudinal model because \(m_1\) must be induced jointly by \(m_2\) and \(g\).

seed_details.one_line: A structural-separation theorem showing finite transition regularity can offset arbitrarily rough first-period propensity estimation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is merely random-design density smoothness under a new name.
seed_details.rebuttal: The transition is a conditional operator that determines a backward regression; it is not a marginal design-density condition.

seed_details.one_line: A slow-rate studentized limit law for sequential projection-HOIF estimators outside the root-n regime.
seed_details.referee_objection: Quadratic U-statistic limit theory already supplies the result.
seed_details.rebuttal: The known theory is single-time and does not establish the variance decomposition after recursive cross-period correction.

seed_details.one_line: Adaptive selection of coupled sequential correction orders and projection dimensions over unknown smoothness indices.
seed_details.referee_objection: Lepski selection directly solves unknown smoothness.
seed_details.rebuttal: The active bottleneck can switch across periods and the sequential mixed remainder makes the tuning problem coupled rather than scalar.

seed_details.one_line: A necessary-and-sufficient scalar-versus-multi-index correction-order frontier for recursive HOIF bias cancellation.
seed_details.referee_objection: Hyperbolic truncation already gives the correction geometry.
seed_details.rebuttal: Its geometry concerns products of variation-independent nuisance errors, not a response estimated from an earlier fitted pseudo-outcome.

seed_details.one_line: A transition-regularity phase diagram separating genuinely compositional bottlenecks from the single-time HOIF submodel.
seed_details.referee_objection: The phase diagram is a direct corollary of a matched-rate theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is retained only as a structural diagnostic and is not the selected standalone kernel.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Cinelli et al. (2025) explicitly state that optimality for time-varying treatment effects outside the root-n regime is open.
Luedtke et al. (2017) derive sequential double robustness from the first-order longitudinal remainder, but do not analyze low-regularity minimax rates.
Robins et al. (2008) develop projection-based HOIF estimators and non-root-n rates for single-time nonlinear functionals.
Robins et al. (2009) show why one-null-versus-mixture lower bounds fail with multiple infinite-dimensional nuisances and introduce two convex mixtures.
Robins et al. (2017) provide higher-order estimating equations with hyperbolic projection truncation for asymmetric nuisance products.
McGrath and Mukherjee (2022) show that sample splitting and nuisance tuning affect functional risk in a single-stage doubly robust problem.
No inspected work derives a recursive HOIF expansion whose correction removes the estimated-pseudo-outcome mixed term.
No inspected work constructs lower-bound alternatives that preserve \(m_1=\int m_2g\) while pairing the transition perturbation with \(\pi_1\).
The selected kernel is therefore the matched-rate theorem; correction architecture and chained lower bounds are indispensable components rather than independent relabelings.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:two-channel-minimax-frontier and dependent statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The proposal is conditional on an exactly state-sufficient, two-period observed-law class with strict overlap, fixed smoothness indices, and iid trajectories; no empirical validation of state sufficiency is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any claimed estimator rate, minimax elbow, separation result, and correction-order result holds only over the stated conditional two-period Markov/state-sufficient model, not under approximate state sufficiency, unknown smoothness, or longer horizons.

banked_reason: D0 mathematics is sound, but D0.5 found field novelty unmet: the incoming r1 lower rate embeds Robins et al. (2009) Theorem 3.1, while the upper and lower rates remain unmatched.
